answer.content:
Interactive DNS Explorer

This tool lets you explore Domain Name System (DNS) concepts interactively. You can:

  • Resolve domains step-by-step, simulating the full DNS lookup process
  • Explore DNS record types with custom inputs
  • Manually edit or create DNS zones and records
  • Explore caching and TTL
  • Simulate DNS query failures and errors
  • Explore DNS delegation, recursion, iterative queries
  • Interact with a curated DNS Query Simulator, Resolver Config, and Zone Editor
DNS Resolver Simulator
Resolver Cache Controls
Resolver Configuration

Customize resolver behavior and policies:

Current Resolution Log
Manual Step Controls
DNS Zone Editor and Configurator

Create, edit, or load DNS zones to use in resolver simulation.

Zone Authority and Delegation Simulation

You can create child zones to simulate delegation. Use 'zone domain' input and load new zones. The root zone is fixed.


    

  






  

    DNS Concepts and Info Explorer
    
Select a DNS concept/term below to get detailed explanations, examples, and interactive mini-demos.

messages.content:
Generate a single, self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript that demonstrates the concept DNS with MAXIMUM interactivity and rich user control. Focus on:

- Multiple interactive controls (buttons, sliders, input fields)
- Complex state transitions and user-driven workflows
- Deep exploration capabilities with many possible interaction paths
- Rich functionality as the PRIMARY goal

IMPORTANT: Keep visual design EXTREMELY MINIMAL and BASIC - use simple black text on white background, basic HTML elements with no styling, no colors, no animations. The interface should be functional but visually plain and unpolished. Prioritize interactive richness over visual appeal.

Only respond with the complete HTML file.
